final_message:
Hello Zara! I am Pal, your friendly math tutor, and I am so excited to learn with you today! We are going to explore something magical called reflection symmetry. 

A line of symmetry is like a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry, making the left and right sides match perfectly. Other shapes have a horizontal line so the top and bottom match, and some shapes even have both! You can use this idea to make beautiful shapes by drawing just half of a picture on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding it. Even letters have symmetry, like the letter A with its vertical line, and the letter H with both. 

Are you ready to find some mirror images together? 🌟

orchestrator_intent.goal: Greet Zara and resume the topic of reflection symmetry.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The session is just starting with no prior messages. The dialogue agent should initiate the conversation.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
